source_jobs.title: VP of Brand Marketing
source_jobs.description:
About GameChanger:

We believe in the life changing impact youth sports have on and off the field. Sports encourage leadership, teamwork, responsibility, and confidence – important life lessons that have the power to propel our youth toward meaningful futures. We recognize that without coaches, parents, and volunteers, organized youth sports could not exist. By building the first and best place to experience the youth sports moments important to our community, we are helping families elevate the next generation through youth sports.

So if you love sports and their community building potential, or building cool products is your sport, GameChanger is the team for you. We are a remote-first, dynamic tech company based in New York City, and we are solving some of the biggest challenges in youth sports today.



The Position:

GameChanger is looking for a Vice President of Brand Marketing to shape and scale our brand as we enter our next phase of growth. You will build brand visibility and relevance, and do so in a way that is modern, authentic, and uniquely GameChanger. This approach will use content, organic social, creators, and community and avoid traditional, high-cost brand tactics. You will own upper-funnel brand strategy, including awareness, consideration, and brand-driven discovery. You will define and steward GameChanger's brand positioning, voice, and creative systems, ensuring they come to life across content, campaigns, and experiences. You will work onsite weekly from our NYC HQ and report to the Chief Commercial Officer and partner with Product, Growth, Communications, and Sales to ensure brand storytelling supports long-term adoption and value.



What You'll Do:

 - Own and evolve GameChanger's brand strategy, including positioning, voice, narrative, and creative systems that scale across channels and teams.

 - Lead upper-funnel brand growth, driving awareness, consideration, and brand-led traffic through compelling storytelling and creative excellence.

 - Build GameChanger into a social-first publisher, creating always-on content that grows followers, impressions, engagement, and share of voice.

 - Oversee influencer and creator marketing, developing authentic partnerships that extend reach and cultural relevance while activating our users as advocates.

 - Lead Integrated Marketing, Social, Content, and Creative teams, ensuring high-quality, on-brand execution and strong cross-functional alignment.

 - Drive measurable acceleration in social audience growth, engagement, and share of voice as primary leading indicators of brand momentum.

 - Own integrated planning and the marketing calendar, aligning launches, campaigns, and content across the business.



Who You Are:

 - 15+ years of experience in brand marketing, including foundational training in a CPG or similarly brand-led organization, combined with experience operating in a high-growth, resource-constrained environment.

 - Demonstrated experience building brands organically through social, creators, and community — with deep platform fluency (algorithms, content mechanics, and influencer ecosystems) and measurable audience growth.

 - Deep experience building consumer-facing brands at scale.

 - Experience defining and scaling brand voice, creative systems, and storytelling frameworks across teams and channels.

 - Experience leading Creative Services, Social Media, Influencer, and Integrated Marketing teams.

 - Comfortable operating with a publisher mindset, working at the speed of culture and modern platforms.

 - Experience managing senior leaders and versatile teams.

 - Passion for youth sports and the communities that surround them.



Perks:

 - Work from our well-furnished, modern office in Manhattan, NY.

 - Unlimited vacation policy.

 - Paid volunteer opportunities.

 - Technology stipend - $4,000 every 2 years after your start to make sure you have the latest and greatest technology.

 - WFH stipend - $500 annually to make your WFH situ

source_jobs.title: Director, Strategic Insights
source_jobs.description:
About GameChanger:

We believe in the life changing impact youth sports have on and off the field. Sports encourage leadership, teamwork, responsibility, and confidence – important life lessons that have the power to propel our youth toward meaningful futures. We recognize that without coaches, parents, and volunteers, organized youth sports could not exist. By building the first and best place to experience the youth sports moments important to our community, we are helping families elevate the next generation through youth sports.



So if you love sports and their community building potential, or building cool products is your sport, GameChanger is the team for you. We are a remote-first, dynamic tech company based in New York City, and we are solving some of the biggest challenges in youth sports today.



About the Role

GameChanger is looking for a Director, Strategic Insights to lead the development of the market, competitive, and business insights that inform the company’s most important strategic decisions. This role will serve as the center of excellence for strategic intelligence across the organization — translating complex market signals, customer behaviors, and competitive dynamics into clear insights that guide GameChanger’s growth and long-term strategy.



As a senior member of the Strategy team, you will partner closely with the Executive Leadership Team onsite at our NYC HQ and senior leaders across Product, Engineering, Marketing, Finance, Data & Analytics, and Business Development. You will shape how the company understands the evolving youth sports landscape, identify emerging opportunities and risks, and ensure leadership has the insights needed to make informed, high-impact decisions.



This role combines rigorous analysis and executive storytelling. You will report to the VP, Strategy, Operations and Impact and establish and lead GameChanger’s strategic insights function — building the frameworks, research capabilities, and intelligence systems that support company-wide planning and strategic decision-making.



What You’ll Do:

Establish and Lead Insights Center of Excellence

 - Own GameChanger’s market intelligence and research agenda, ensuring leadership has a clear view of industry trends, competitive dynamics, and emerging opportunities.

 - Lead primary and secondary research initiatives, including quantitative surveys, qualitative research, industry analysis, and market sizing.

 - Develop frameworks to systematically track competitors, new entrants, product innovation, and structural shifts across the youth sports and sports technology landscape.

 - Source and manage external research partners, data providers, and consultants to deliver high-quality strategic insights.

 - Build institutional knowledge of the youth sports ecosystem and adjacent industries to inform strategic decision-making.
   

Deliver Executive-Level Strategic Insights

 - Translate complex research, data, and market signals into clear insights and strategic implications for executive leadership.

 - Prepare executive-ready analyses, presentations, and strategic narratives that inform leadership discussions and planning processes.

 - Identify key risks, opportunities, and inflection points that could shape GameChanger’s long-term growth trajectory.

 - Provide leadership with ongoing perspectives on customer behavior, competitive positioning, and industry evolution.
   

Inform Company Strategy & Growth Decisions

 - Partner closely with the Strategy team to inform corporate strategy, product strategy, monetization strategy, and growth initiatives.

 - Serve as a strategic advisor to GMs and business leaders, operating as an internal consultant to diagnose key business questions, design research and analysis, and deliver actionable insights that inform decisions.

 - Conduct customer segmentation, behavioral analysis, and needs assessments to guide strategic priorities.

 - Support long-range planning and strategic decis

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Video Enablement
source_jobs.description:
About GameChanger:

We believe in the life-changing impact youth sports have on and off the field because they encourage leadership, teamwork, responsibility, and confidence—important life lessons that have the power to propel our youth toward meaningful futures. We recognize that without coaches, parents, and volunteers, organized youth sports could not exist. By building the first and best place to experience the youth sports moments important to our community, we are helping families elevate the next generation through youth sports.

So if you love sports and their community-building potential, or building cool products is your sport, GameChanger is the team for you. We are a remote-first, dynamic tech company based in New York City, and we are solving some of the biggest challenges in youth sports today.



The Position:

GameChanger users already live stream and watch millions of hours of youth sporting events and clips using the GameChanger app, and we’re looking for a fully-remote Staff Software Engineer/Architect to help lead the expansion of our video capabilities as we form our new Video Engineering team. While we’ve had teams build video functionality, this full-stack, empowered team will be dedicated to video - owning our video pipeline end to end, evolving our live streaming and post-game video capabilities for users, simplifying the developer experience for product teams that are integrating video into our apps, and ensuring we have a scalable and resilient platform to handle millions of streams and hundreds of millions of highlight clips annually.



As Staff Software Engineer, you’ll be a hands-on architect and software engineer leading development efforts within the team, influencing direction, roadmaps, and technical direction. A strong communicator and mentor, you’ll collaborate closely with the Engineering Manager and Product Manager on technical roadmap and strategy, mentor and pair with other developers, and collaborate with other platform and product teams to design and build video-related projects that advance our streaming capabilities, handle our increasing scale, and improve our users’ experience with video to ensure that fans can always capture and watch the moments that matter to them most. As a founding member of this new team, you’ll help set the tone and culture, onboard other engineers, and influence the entire video stack: mobile broadcast and playback frameworks, back-end ingest/transcoding/playback infrastructure, VoD storage and playback, and hooks for other workflows such as automatic highlight clipping, advertising, live-stream overlays, and AI/Computer Vision workflows.

Check out some of our current video capabilities at https://gc.com/live-video



What You’ll Do:

 - Working in your cross-functional team of mobile, web, back-end, and infrastructure engineers, you’ll design, architect, code, and support video features across the video pipeline stack, including live video ingestion, transcoding, integration, storage, and playback/consumption at scale.

 - Working closely with the Engineering Manager and Product Manager of the video team, you’ll help influence, communicate, and iterate on the strategic roadmap for the technology powering our video features as we continue to scale and add new capabilities.

 - Collaborate closely with mobile, web, machine learning/computer vision, and platform teams on multi-faceted projects across the entire video processing system to drive improved user experiences and business outcomes.

 - Review and provide feedback on designs and code PRs, pair on architectures, and provide guidance and hands-on help as needed.

 - Help to build and connect to internal SDKs, frameworks, and tooling to enable product development teams to easily integrate video into the product at various stages of the video pipeline.

 - Help define and build real-time monitoring and measurement systems for critical operational workflows in the video pipeline, including tools for on-

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
About GameChanger:

We believe in the life-changing impact youth sports have on and off the field. Sports encourage leadership, teamwork, responsibility, and confidence – important life lessons that have the power to propel our youth toward meaningful futures. We recognize that without coaches, parents, and volunteers, organized youth sports could not exist. By building the first and best place to experience the youth sports moments important to our community, we are helping families elevate the next generation through youth sports.



So if you love sports and their community building potential, or building cool products is your sport, GameChanger is the team for you. We are a remote-first, dynamic tech company based in New York City, and we are solving some of the biggest challenges in youth sports today.



About the Role

GameChanger is looking for a Senior Product Marketing Manager to join our growing team. In this role, you’ll own the go-to-market strategy for some of our newest product investments, helping define how new experiences come to market and drive adoption across coaches, parents, athletes, and organizations.

This is an embedded product marketing role. You’ll partner closely with Product Managers, Product Designers, and Data team from early product discovery through launch—bringing customer, competitive, and market insights into product decisions, shaping positioning and messaging, and ensuring every launch delivers meaningful customer value.

We’re looking for someone who enjoys building where there isn’t yet a playbook. You’ll thrive if you’re energized by ambiguity, comfortable influencing cross-functional teams without direct authority, and excited to help define both the product experience and the go-to-market strategy in a fast-moving, collaborative environment. This role works on a hybrid basis in our NYC HQ and reports to the Director of Product Marketing and sits within our Commercial organization.



What You'll Do:

 - Define and execute go-to-market strategy for your assigned product area, including segmentation, cross-functional alignment, and planning across quarterly and annual cycles.

 - Identify and close gaps in user, industry, and competitive knowledge, leading research to surface opportunities and sharpen GTM strategy.

 - Own positioning and messaging, ensuring differentiation across segments and alignment with our company vision.

 - Serve as an embedded strategic partner to Product Managers, Designers, and Data & Analytics throughout the product development lifecycle—not just at launch. Bring customer, competitive, and market insights into roadmap discussions, influence product direction, shape user experiences, and ensure products are positioned for successful adoption before they ship.

 - Drive feature adoption by identifying and activating the right marketing channels, partnerships, and GTM motions.

 - Measure GTM effectiveness across launches and adoption campaigns, identify gaps, and lead iterative improvements to strategy and execution.



Who You Are:

 - 5+ years of product marketing experience in B2B and/or B2C within high-growth companies.

 - Experience bringing new products or features from 0→1—creating positioning, defining go-to-market strategy, and building processes where no established playbook existed.

 - Experience launching entirely new products, business lines, or customer experiences from concept through adoption.

 - Experience in consumer subscription products, mobile applications, creator/community platforms, sports technology, or other consumer technology businesses.

 - Strong strategic thinker who can translate user insights, market context, and product vision into clear positioning, messaging, and GTM plans.

 - Strong communication, executive presence, and influencing skills with the ability to build trust and manage positive cross-functional relationships with internal stakeholders and external partners.

 - Comfortable operating in ambiguity and build

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager, Product Strategy & Operations
source_jobs.description:
About GameChanger:

We believe in the life-changing impact youth sports have on and off the field because they encourage leadership, teamwork, responsibility, and confidence—important life lessons that have the power to propel our youth toward meaningful futures. We recognize that without coaches, parents, and volunteers, organized youth sports could not exist. By building the first and best place to experience the youth sports moments important to our community, we are helping families elevate the next generation through youth sports.



So if you love sports and their community-building potential, or building cool products is your sport, GameChanger is the team for you. We are a remote-first, dynamic tech company based in New York City, and we are solving some of the biggest challenges in youth sports today.



The Position:

As GameChanger continues to grow, we are looking for a Senior Manager, Product Strategy & Operations to partner with our Chief Product & Technology Officer in scaling the operating model behind our Product organization. In this role, you will help define and improve the planning processes, operating rhythms, prioritization frameworks, and reporting mechanisms that enable Product teams to execute effectively and remain focused on the highest-impact opportunities. You will serve as a strategic operational partner across Product, Engineering, Design, Data, and Commercial teams, helping drive alignment, visibility, and accountability across key initiatives. This is a highly cross-functional individual contributor role for a strong operator who enjoys bringing structure to ambiguity, translating strategy into execution, and helping organizations operate more effectively. You will report directly to the Chief Product & Technology Officer and work from our New York City HQ.



What You’ll Do:

 - Define and continuously improve the Product operating model, including planning cycles, roadmap reviews, prioritization frameworks, leadership cadences, and execution rhythms

 - Lead annual, quarterly, and ongoing planning processes to ensure alignment between company objectives, product priorities, and team execution

 - Build and maintain portfolio-level visibility across Product initiatives through operational dashboards, KPI reporting, roadmap tracking, and dependency management

 - Partner with Product, Engineering, Design, Data, Finance, and Commercial stakeholders to improve cross-functional alignment, execution consistency, and organizational focus

 - Establish and manage operational reporting processes that help leadership understand progress, risks, trade-offs, and strategic opportunities

 - Translate customer feedback, product metrics, and organizational signals into actionable recommendations that improve prioritization and decision-making

 - Drive leadership communications through all-hands content, portfolio reviews, planning materials, executive updates, and organizational storytelling

 - Identify operational inefficiencies and implement scalable processes, tools, and workflows that improve visibility, accountability, and execution across the Product organization



Who You Are:

 - 8+ years of experience in Product Operations, Strategy & Operations, Program Management, Product Management, Business Operations, or a related field within a technology organization

 - Experience building and scaling operating models, planning processes, roadmap governance, or portfolio management functions within Product organizations

 - Experience leading cross-functional initiatives and influencing stakeholders across Product, Engineering, Design, Data, and business teams

 - Experience developing KPI frameworks, operational reporting, dashboards, or portfolio visibility processes that support executive decision-making

 - Strong understanding of product development lifecycles, roadmap planning, prioritization frameworks, and organizational operating rhythms

 - Experience working directly with senior leaders in fa

source_jobs.title: Director, Information Security & Technology
source_jobs.description:
About GameChanger:

We believe in the life changing impact youth sports have on and off the field. Sports encourage leadership, teamwork, responsibility, and confidence – important life lessons that have the power to propel our youth toward meaningful futures. We recognize that without coaches, parents, and volunteers, organized youth sports could not exist. By building the first and best place to experience the youth sports moments important to our community, we are helping families elevate the next generation through youth sports.



So if you love sports and their community building potential, or building cool products is your sport, GameChanger is the team for you. We are a remote-first, dynamic tech company based in New York City, and we are solving some of the biggest challenges in youth sports today.



The Position:

GameChanger's Information Security and Technology organization protects the products, systems, and data that the families, coaches, and young athletes who rely on us trust us with every day, and keeps the internal technology our employees depend on running smoothly. We treat security and technology as business enablers - our role is to help GameChanger move fast and grow safely.



As Director of Information Security and Technology, you will own the strategy, team, and multi-year roadmap across Information Security and IT Operations. You will lead a multidisciplinary organization responsible for product and application security, cloud security, AI security, security operations, vulnerability management, governance, risk, compliance, and internal technology support. You will lead through managers, set technical and organizational direction, and partner closely with Engineering, Product, Legal, People, Finance, executive leadership, and teams across the business to embed security and sound technology practices into how GameChanger operates.



GameChanger is a subsidiary of Dick's Sporting Goods (DSG). You will represent GameChanger security within DSG, partnering with the DSG CISO and enterprise security organization to align tooling and processes while retaining ownership and autonomy of Security and Operations for GameChanger’s environment. You will report to the Senior Director of Engineering at GameChanger.



What You'll Do:

 - Own and evolve GameChanger's multi-year security and technology strategy, including prioritization, resourcing decisions, alignment to business and parent company goals, and communication of security investments and tradeoffs to technical and executive leadership.

 - Lead, mentor, and grow a team of security and technology operations professionals, including setting priorities, coaching teammates, managing performance, creating opportunities for growth and impact, and creating an environment to support autonomy and ownership.

 - Own the direction and delivery for all key areas of information security, including product and application security, cloud security, AI security, security operations, vulnerability management, and governance, risk, and compliance functions.

 - Evaluate and mitigate risks across infrastructure, product, and corporate environments.

 - Set direction for AI security and governance as part of a secure SDLC and corporate environment, including the secure adoption of AI tooling, the security of AI-enabled product features, and AI usage governance, partnering with AI enablement and governance stakeholders across the company.

 - Lead the governance, risk, and compliance program, including internal assessments (for example, NIST CSF 2.0), external assessments, and clear risk reporting to leadership.

 - Own the security operations and incident response program for the GameChanger environment, including detection, on-call, escalation, and coordination with our parent-company security function.

 - Own internal technology operations, including the technology support function for our remote-first workforce and the systems and endpoints employees rely on to do

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Basketball
source_jobs.description:
About GameChanger:

We believe in the life changing impact youth sports have on and off the field. Sports encourage leadership, teamwork, responsibility, and confidence – important life lessons that have the power to propel our youth toward meaningful futures. We recognize that without coaches, parents, and volunteers, organized youth sports could not exist. By building the first and best place to experience the youth sports moments important to our community, we are helping families elevate the next generation through youth sports.



So if you love sports and their community building potential, or building cool products is your sport, GameChanger is the team for you. We are a remote first, dynamic tech company based in New York City, and we are solving some of the biggest challenges in youth sports today.



The Position:

Basketball is one of GameChanger’s fastest-growing strategic investments. As Senior Product Manager, you’ll help shape the future of our Basketball experience, defining product strategy to launching entirely new capabilities for teams, coaches, and families. This is a highly cross-functional, builder role where you’ll partner closely with Engineering, Design, Strategy, Marketing, and Business Development to create products from the ground up. You will report to one of our Product Management Directors.



What You'll Do:

Define Product Strategy

 - Partner across Product, Engineering, Design, Strategy, Marketing, and Business Development to shape the long-term vision and strategy for GameChanger Basketball.

 - Identify opportunities through customer research, market trends, competitive insights, and business priorities.

 - Make thoughtful tradeoffs that balance customer needs with business impact.

Lead Product Development

 - Own the product lifecycle from discovery and ideation through launch, measurement, and ongoing iteration.

 - Translate strategy into a clear roadmap and work with Engineering and Design to deliver top-notch customer experiences.

 - Launch quickly, learn from customer feedback, and continuously improve your product.

Drive Customer & Business Outcomes

 - Define success metrics and use qualitative and quantitative insights to evaluate product performance.

 - Partner with Analytics to uncover opportunities, validate hypotheses, and inform future investments.

 - Ensure product decisions contribute to customer satisfaction, engagement, and business growth.

Collaborate & Influence

 - Communicate product vision, priorities, milestones, and outcomes to stakeholders at all levels of the organization.

 - Foster alignment across cross-functional teams while navigating ambiguity and competing priorities.



Who You Are:

 - 5+ years of product management experience building and shipping consumer-facing mobile products, ideally within sports, family technology, gaming, subscriptions, or other high-engagement consumer experiences.

 - Proven ability to lead products from discovery through launch, using customer insights, experimentation, and data to prioritize investments.

 - Experience partnering closely with Engineering and Design to deliver intuitive, high-quality customer experiences.

 - Strong product judgment with the ability to balance customer needs, technical complexity, and business objectives when making tradeoffs.

 - Comfortable operating in fast-paced, ambiguous environments where you’re helping define both the problem and the solution.

 - Skilled at influencing cross-functional partners and aligning stakeholders without direct authority.

 - Excellent communication skills, with experience presenting product strategy, priorities, and outcomes to audiences across all levels of an organization.

 - Naturally curious and customer-obsessed, with a bias toward action and a passion for building products that families love.

 - Knowledge of or passion for basketball and a desire to help shape the future of the sport through technology is preferred.



Perks:

 - Work remotely t

notes: GameChanger is a youth sports platform with a mix of tech and non-tech roles.
